Issue 1.1 – March 2026 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_CLIMATE_OHC_IBI_area_averaged_anomalies  
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
The Iberia-Biscay-Ireland (IBI) Ocean Heat Content (OHC) indicator provides annually 
averaged, spatially averaged time series of OHC for the IBI region (26°N –56°N, 19°W–
5°E), along with the associated error bars. 
 
The error bars of the product are derived from the computation of the same parameter 
using a set of gridded Copernicus products available for the region (see Section 1.c). 
 
For each specific ensemble product, OHC is computed following the methodology 
described in Lima et al. (2020) and Mayer et al. (2021), using the following equation: 
 
2
0 p mth clim
1
z
OHC = ρ c (T - T )dz
z

 
 
where Tclim corresponds to the average temperature over entire data record, including 
all available temporal record, T mth corresponds to the temperature averaged for a 
specific month. 
A reference density of 0 = 1030 kg·m-3 and a specific heat capacity of cp = 3980 J/kg°C 
were used (e.g. von Schuckmann et al., 2009). This variable is closely proportional to the 
average temperature change from z1 = 0 m to z2 = 2000 m depth.
